highlights: Final resting place of over 10,000 soldiers who died defending the Ho Chi Minh Trail during the Vietnam War
Vast cemetery organized into sections by province, with graves arranged in perfect alignment across hillside
National monument built 1975-1977 on Ben Tat hill in Gio Linh, Quang Tri with temple at center

thingsToKnow.crowds: Attracts thousands of visitors annually, especially on July 27 (Invalids and Martyrs Day)
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Cemetery spans large area; exploring all sections thoroughly may require 1.5 days
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Visit during cooler mornings or late afternoons; bring light rain gear as weather can be unpredictable

visitorTips: Bring flowers or incense to pay respects; many graves lack burial remains due to wartime losses
Plan extended time to explore; over 10,000 graves make it impossible to see everything quickly
Visit with contemplative mindset; maintain quiet, respectful demeanor rather than treating as typical tourist attraction

faq.question: How many soldiers are buried here?
faq.answer: Over 10,263 soldiers are buried here, primarily those who died on the Ho Chi Minh Trail. Many graves are memorial markers without actual remains.

faq.question: How long does it take to visit?
faq.answer: Exploring the entire cemetery thoroughly can take 1.5 days. Most visitors spend 1-2 hours in main areas due to the vast size.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Visit early morning or late afternoon. July 27 (Martyrs Day) draws thousands. Bring rain protection as weather is unpredictable.

sentiment.positive: Visitors find the cemetery deeply moving and solemn, with well-maintained grounds that inspire gratitude and respect for wartime sacrifices
